observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to spearhead the planning and the conduct of physical count of all PPE in the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU and COA Circular No. 2020-006.  

Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to prepare PPELC and Property Cards, respectively, and to regularly reconcile the balances thereof.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: 1. Make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to reclassify the remaining completed projects currently recorded under the CIP accounts to the correct PPE accounts; 2. Compute the corresponding depreciation expense, prior period adjustments relative thereto, and accumulated depreciation for all completed projects; 3. Coordinate with the Acting Municipal Engineer to obtain updated information on the status of ongoing projects to ensure proper classification of the asset accounts as of year-end; and b. Direct the Acting Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with copies of the Report of Completed Projects or Report on Government Projects/Programs/Activities with necessary supporting documents, to ensure accurate presentation of related accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to serve as the basis for the set-up of the correct RPT/SET Receivables in the books; and b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the recording of receivables and reconcile the accounting records with the Municipal Treasurer’s reports and immediately effect the necessary corrections and adjustments.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned personnel to: a. Ensure strict adherence to the prescribed Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for inventories; b. Immediately conduct a physical inventory count of all supplies, materials, and semi-expendable properties, and submit the required Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); c. Ensure proper recognition of inventory as assets upon acquisition and reclassification to expense only upon issuance or use; d. Maintain and regularly update Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards for each type of supply item to ensure proper tracking of receipts and issuances of inventories; e. Strengthen coordination between General Servicer Office (GSO) and Accounting Office to ensure accuracy and consistency in inventory reporting; f. Provide training for key personnel on inventory management and government accounting procedures; and g. Make the necessary adjustments to reflect correct and reliable inventory balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Recognize and record accumulated depreciation for all existing Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) accounts following IPSAS; b. Compute and properly apply the correct recognition of depreciation expense for PPE accounts for the current year; and c. Make the necessary prior period adjustments to accurately reflect their balances in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer's Office (MTO) and other concerned personnel to: a. Strictly comply with the legal posting and publication requirements for Notices of Delinquencies as stated in Section 254 of RA 7160; and b. Proactively explore and implement all feasible methods to improve tax collection efficiency and ensure that BLGF targets are met.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Remit immediately the CY 2025 contributions amounting to ₱61,213.69 to GSIS and ₱68,089.51 to PhilHealth; b. Submit promptly any changes in salary to the agencies so that accurate figures will be reflected on their statements to the Municipality; c. Perform a thorough reconciliation and review of source documents to accurately identify the unremitted inter-agency payables; d. Upon completion of reconciliation, immediately remit the identified balances to the appropriate government agencies; and e. Regularly reconcile the inter-agency payables to ensure timely and complete remittance of all obligations. We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned personnel to promptly submit their identification/policy numbers to ensure timely remittance of their contributions.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Verify if some of the items purchased were reusable and have been returned to storage, and prepare necessary adjusting entries, such as debiting to semi-expendable inventory accounts; and b. Ensure the proper recording of the transactions under the Repairs and Maintenance-Building and Other Structures Account.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002, specifically the transfer of all unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE balances to the Trust Fund at year-end; b. Ensure timely and accurate recording of transactions in both the General Fund and Trust Fund books, in line with IPSAS 1; c. Review and reconcile DRRMF-related transactions quarterly, to detect discrepancies early and ensure proper fund classification and reporting; and d. Correct prior period misstatements by preparing adjusting entries in FY 2025, clearly disclosing them in the notes to the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the MPDO, Municipal Engineer and all concerned personnel to thoroughly plan the projects, programs, and activities funded under the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC to ensure their timely implementation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned officials/personnel to: a. Immediately submit the lacking supporting documents for the travel claims already paid to Job Order personnel, particularly the certification from the Head of the Agency or any authorized approving official, in compliance with the requirements set forth under COA Resolution No. 2021-044; b. Ensure strict compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Resolution No. 2021-044 by requiring that all necessary supporting documents, including the certification from the Head of Agency or authorized official-are properly secured before the processing and payment of any future travel claims for Job Order personnel; c. Review and update internal policies or create supplemental guidelines to reflect the requirements of COA Resolution No. 2021-044; and d. Strengthen internal controls and implement measures to ensure that disbursements are properly documented, duly authorized, and fully compliant with applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned officials/personnel to: a. Ensure timely submission of its GPB to secure proper DILG endorsement in the succeeding fiscal year; b. Strengthen the monitoring and evaluation mechanism of the GAD Focal Point System to ensure timely and full implementation of planned PPAs; and c. Capacitate GAD personnel on compliance with JMC provisions, gender analysis, and GAD budgeting and reporting standards when necessary.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Ensure strict compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by mandating the timely preparation and monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF; b. Closely coordinate to meet the prescribed deadlines; and c. Device a monitoring and internal control mechanisms to prevent future delays and ensure adherence to COA reporting requirements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Inventory Committee will spearhead the planning and the conduct of physical count of all PPE owned by the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to the provisions stipulated in COA Circular Nos. 2020-006, 92-386 and Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU. 
	
Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed to prepare the PPELCs and the General Services Officer to take charge in the monitoring of issuances and transfers of PPE, to prepare Property Cards and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records regularly.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the Source Agencies and prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted; and


Implement the prompt and proper liquidation of trust funds received from Source Agencies, including the return of excess/unutilized funds.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the HRMO to assess the existing organizational structure of the Municipality and to evaluate the manpower needs of all departments to ensure optimum delivery of service to its constituents;



Review and examine the tasks assigned to each job order personnel to preclude duplication in assignments; and 

Adopt merit and incentive award strategies to motivate all personnel and to enhance their potentials in performing their functions.

We recommended and the Management agreed to cause the immediate liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the related provisions of the law.



We also recommended and the Management agreed to:

Send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30 day period specified under Section 14 of EO 298 for official local travel a written reminder under signature of the head of agency or his authorized representative, enjoining the official or employee concerned to liquidate his/her travel cash advance/s, pursuant to Section 3.3.2 (a) of COA Circular No. 96-004;

Remove the name of the official or employee from the subsequent payrolls until such time that the cash advance has been fully liquidated, if the official/ employee concerned fails to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.3.2(b) of the same circular; and

Refrain from granting additional cash advance to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended and the Management agreed to adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically in the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF so that proper utilization, recording and releases of the fund could be verified on a timely manner and that proper charging and recording can be effected.

We recommended and the Management agreed to revisit the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and implement the same in accordance with Republic Act No. (RA) 9003.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to enforce the closure and rehabilitation of the controlled dumpsite to ensure protection of public health and environment.

We recommended and the Management agreed to use the Petty Cash Fund only for those recurring petty operating expenses and to comply with the documentary requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 for its disbursements in order to fully establish the validity of claims to the Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its revised IRR. 


We likewise recommended and the Management agreed that payments for supplies and materials as well as other operating expenses be made directly to suppliers in order to properly deduct/withhold the mandatory taxes due to the national government.

We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the supplier to recover the overpayment amounting to ₱4,223.29.


We further recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that all documents are thoroughly reviewed and verified before they are submitted for approval by the LCE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties and equipment before losing its value due to rapid deterioration considering the length of time that it had been unserviceable.




We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the proper accounting of unserviceable properties with regard to account classification pursuant to Section 140 of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume III, to ensure presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials and employees to:

Submit the contract agreement/MOA/ suspension orders/ resume orders to determine whether or not the contractor is liable to pay liquidated damages prior to rescindment of the contract in addition to the forfeiture of the contractor’s performance security;

Complete the remaining unfinished scope of work of the project to avoid deterioration of the structure and for the beneficiary barangays to enjoy the benefits therefrom; and



Familiarize themselves with the pertinent provisions prescribed by R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and ensure that all the procurement activities made by the Municipality be in accordance with the same.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and other officials concerned to submit the lacking documents identified in Appendix F.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and other officials concerned to submit the enumerated lacking documents identified in 2020 AAR Observation No. 11.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned personnel to provide a written justification for the discrepancies noted and submit the lacking documents needed for proper inspection of the infrastructure project and evaluation of the validity and propriety of the transactions involved pursuant to the above-mentioned guidelines.
We further recommended that the concerned Municipal officials and employees cause the refund of the 2019 Service Recognition Incentive (SRI) totaling ₱560,049.06.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a repair and maintenance record of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipality in accordance with Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Circular No. 81-131-A dated January 29, 1981 to avoid unnecessary expenses for the repair and maintenance.

We further recommended that the damaged and unserviceable properties and equipment be kept in a safe stockroom located within the premises of the Municipality to properly safeguard it against loss, wastage and unauthorized use and for easy determination and retrieval of properties to be disposed of.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile records to facilitate remittance of prior years’ balances and provide a detailed breakdown of the balances of each trust liabilities as of December 31, 2019 so as to identify the period and names of personnel these balances are attributed to.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees cause the refund of the 2018 bonuses amounting to ₱1.495 million.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees cause the refund of the total amount of ₱198,626.58 representing the traveling expenses incurred.
We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the 2017 bonuses amounting to ₱2.522 million.
We recommended the refund of ₱270,000.00 paid to the legal consultant being an irregular expense.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees cause the refund of the total amount of ₱596,200.00 representing the traveling expenses incurred on Lakbay Aral.
We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the PEI for CY 2015 amounting to ₱4.210 million.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees cause the refund of the total amount of ₱0.825 million representing the traveling expenses incurred on Lakbay Aral.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay as prescribed under the GAA of 2014 be observed effective January 2015 and onwards.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board instruct /order the refund of Cash Gift paid to Local School Board Volunteer Teachers and Watchmen.
We recommended that bonus and cash gift paid to Liga staffs amounting to ₱17,500.00 be refunded for lack of legal basis.
We recommended that all identified Municipal Officials cause the refund of the total amount of ₱92,000.00 representing the cost of eight (8) units Galaxy Tablet PC.
We recommended that all Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the 2014 Productivity Enhancement Incentive amounting to ₱2,334,400.00.
Refund the additional bonus of ₱1,027,000.00 granted to elected officials, regular and casual employees on December 2013.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and the Supply Office reconcile their records for Buildings. As an initial step, the Supply Office should maintain Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment in accordance with Section 119 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Volume I. The Accounting Office, in accordance with Section 120 of the same manual should maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards. The accounting and property records should be reconciled regularly. Any discrepancy noted should be adjusted accordingly.
We recommended that concerned official exert effort to determine cost of all service vehicles so that these will be properly recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that offers submitted by prospective suppliers or bidders include, among others, brand names of goods. Also, no other information shall form part of the contract or Purchase Orders apart from the specifications contained in the offer of the supplier or bidder.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Stop the charging of monthly wages of municipal job hires and expenses for bloodletting activity against the LDRRM Fund; and 

Properly program the LDRRMFIP for efficient and effective utilization of LDRRMF in conformity with Item 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure optimum utilization of the LDRRMF through proper planning, prioritization and fast-tracking the implementation of priority projects, programs and activities to afford its constituents the readiness of resources in times of disaster.

We recommended and the Management agreed to review the previous journal entries made for the utilization of the trust funds. Also, a thorough analysis of the Due to LGU account shall be made, segregating proper accounts and their balances even that of prior periods. 

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should henceforward record the purchases of construction materials for development projects by administration as inventory and the consumption thereof in the appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the list of recipients to support the liquidation on the issuances of the various supplies received.

Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed to record the purchases of inventories in the appropriate inventory accounts and the consumption thereof as expense, in accordance with IPSAS 12. Also, subsequent issuance of the same should be supported with RIS.

We recommended and the Management agreed to henceforward make a schedule of computation as to the share of the Barangays on real property tax collections of the Municipality in accordance with Section 271, Chapter VII, Title 2 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the lacking documents in order to further validate the propriety of the disbursements. 

We also recommended and the Management agreed to fully comply with the filing and submission of the documentary requirements set forth in RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned personnel to indicate in the Purchase Orders the complete detailed specifications of the items procured in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in sending written notice to the payee of the existence of the check.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all department heads to submit at the beginning of the year, the list of all on-going government projects/ programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year together with the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Projects/ Programs/ Activities for the quarter ending March 31, June 30, September 30 and December 31, 2019 and years thereafter to ensure compliance with laws, rules and regulations particularly COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We also recommended that the department head concerned inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already posted for validation.

We recommended that the officials concerned submit, together with the GAD Accomplishment Report, the documents enumerated above to determine the appropriate amount of the programs/ projects/activities attributed to GAD budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to submit regularly to the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for verification purposed to determine the reasonableness of fuel and lubricants consumed during the period and to effectively control fuel, gasoline and oil expenditures and avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of the Official Receipts on remittances already made from the two Cooperatives to avoid suspension and or disallowance of the said transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct concerned personnel to submit immediately the required documents enumerated in order that the authenticity and legality of the procurement could be evaluated accordingly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to henceforward recognize as inventory the supplies and materials for consumption or distribution upon receipt and subsequent issuance of the same should be supported with Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) in order to properly account for the inventories of the Municipality.

properties and to provide the corresponding depreciation using the determined

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other officials concerned to be specific in identifying infrastructure projects for implementation in case of those funds intended to assist the Barangays and requiring the latter to submit requests beforehand so that certain amount could be appropriated for them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other officials concerned to be thorough in identifying programs, projects and activities in order to maximize the purpose for which any fund is established to ensure that the Municipality’s constituents could fully avail of the benefits to which they are entitled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Bids and Awards Committee to impose upon winning bidders for procurement of goods the submission of performance securities in compliance with Republic Act No. 9184 and to ensure faithful performance of contractors of the terms and conditions of their contracts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop drawing cash advances from the funds of the Municipality for purposes other than travelling expenses.


We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions stated under Section 339 of the Local Government Code (RA No. 7160) and COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting cash advances to any official or employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Development Council, Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to prepare a monitoring schedule so as to prioritize the timely and full implementation of all programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to prioritize the use of LDRRMF and prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP) in accordance with laws, rules and regulations to support disaster mitigation, preparation, response, rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned personnel to provide a written justification for the discrepancies noted and submit the lacking documents needed for proper inspection of the infrastructure project and evaluation of the validity and propriety of the transactions involved pursuant to the above-mentioned guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned personnel to perform the following:

a. 	Prepare an Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan and formulate policies and guidelines so that the necessity and propriety of hiring job order employees are justified.


b. 	Incorporate in the contract the specific services to be rendered and the number of man hours, and quantity and duration of the works to be performed by the job order personnel and require them to submit quantified accomplishment reports.

c. 	Assess and review the necessity of hiring job orders by considering the existence of regular employees who could perform some of the contracted work. Maximize the services of the regular employees and minimize the hiring of job order personnel.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly remit the amounts due to the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and Philhealth within the required remittance period to avoid penalties and surcharges pursuant to Section 69 of PD 1445, Section 6(b) of RA 8291, RA 7875 as amended by RA 9241) and RA 7742.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile records to facilitate remittance of prior years’ balances and provide a detailed breakdown of the balances of each trust liabilities as of December 31, 2019 so as to identify the period and names of personnel these balances are attributed to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all officials and employees concerned to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Failure of the Accountable Officer to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute for the withholding of his salary and the instruction of other sanctions. 
 
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant furnish the Municipal Mayor as well the department heads concerned, a listing of long-outstanding unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enforce its liquidation/settlement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to secure the Memorandum of Agreement/Contract for the fund transfers received as a guide in the disposition and implementation of the projects as well as to comply with the documentary requirements provided in Section 3.1.2 of COA Cir. No. 2012-001.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and other personnel concerned to submit monthly to the Auditor the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursement to report the utilization of fund in accordance with Sections 4.6 and 6.5 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other personnel concerned revisit Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160, Budget Circular No. 2017-02, Budget Circular No. 2016-04, and Budget Circular No. 2017-04 and should refrain from incurring additional PS expenses/cost until such time that the limitation is observed. The implementation of any increase in PS cost should be held in abeyance until the municipality shall not exceed PS limitation/cap.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to change the lay-out of the office so that clients can transact business through outside windows to properly safeguard cash, records, documents and equipment against possible loss, theft or robbery and in compliance with sound internal control.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations on the procurement of goods and services.

We recommended that the Municipality submit GAD Accomplishment Report duly reviewed by DILG with the documents enumerated to determine the efficiency and effectiveness of programs/projects and activities.
We recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials/employees/personnel submit the required supporting documents as stipulated in Annex III.G and to refund immediately excessive travel claims totaling P18,960.00

We recommended  that the Municipal Administrator/Municipal Engineer:

Submit regularly to the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel and lubricants consumed during the period and to effectively control fuel, gasoline and oil expenditures and avoid wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prepare budget proposals for the development of Sanitary Landfill including PPAs relative thereto to be included in the succeeding year’s annual budget.

Meanwhile, we encourage the municipality to observe proper waste segregation and disposal to prevent spreading of waste items around the dumpsite.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay as prescribed under the GAA of 2014 be observed effective January 2015 and onwards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with the Liga Board to reduce the rates of wages of Contractuals/Job Hires assigned thereat to be at par with the rates given by the Municipality.  We likewise recommended to reduce the number of staff so that funds will be utilized to more worthwhile projects. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

2. Require the rendition of overtime services by regular employees in the exigency of the service and to meet reports deadlines.
3. Adopt merit and incentive award strategies to motivate all personnel and to enhance their potentials in performing their functions.

Management   should   refrain   from   incurring   additional    PS expenses/cost until such time that the limitation is observed.  The implementation of any  increase  in  PS  cost  should  be  held  in abeyance until the municipality shall not exceed PS limitation/cap.

Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98, dated October 14, 2011.
Intensify tax collection efforts to increase the municipality’s income.
Refrain from creating new items for plantilla of personnel so as to lessen Personal Services expenses.
Refund the  additional  bonus  of  P1,027,000.00  granted  to  elected officials, regular and casual employees on December, 2013.

We recommended that LGU management refrain  from  hiring  more  job order personnel since it  has  exceeded   the  limits  of  personal services for this category. Also, we  recommended  that  management review and examine the tasks assigned to each job  order/casual to preclude duplication in assignments.  We recommended  further   the termination of  services  of  employees  assigned  to  clerical  or administrative in  nature  or  where  the  work  is  already  being performed by the regular personnel. The hiring of  security  guards for the schools and the unnecessary close-in security of the Mayor should be stopped and the current ones terminated.

The Municipal Mayor should create  a  committee to establish a repair and maintenance program on proper maintenance of all government properties.  The  conduct  of  inventory  of  all municipal vehicles  and  verification  of  their  actual  condition should be prioritized.  If  they  can  no  longer  be  repaired  or reconditioned or whose maintenance costs  of  repair  outweigh  the benefits and services that will be  derived  from  their  continued use, or when the repair cost is more than thirty per cent (30%)  of its fair market  value,  the property  is  no  longer  economically repairable and should be disposed of in accordance with Section  79 of PD 1445.

It is recommended that the Accounting Office and the Supply Office reconcile their records for Buildings.  As an initial step, the Supply Office should maintain Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment in accordance with Section 119 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Volume 1.   The Accounting Office, in accordance  with  Section  120  of  the  same manual should maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards.   The accounting and property records  should  be reconciled regularly.  Any discrepancy noted should be adjusted accordingly.

We recommended  that the Supply Officer take the necessary  actions to have the unserviceable vehicles be  disposed  of  in  accordance with the procedures enumerated in Title V- Sections 162-193 of  COA Circular No. 92-386.  The cost of P2,355,000.00 should  be  dropped from book of accounts once these vehicles are disposed of.
We recommended that concerned official exert  effort  to  determine cost of all  Service  Vehicles  so  that  these  will  be  properly recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local School Board:
Stop the practice of hiring volunteer teachers and allowing them to teach in any grade levels, but to work instead for the organization and operation of extension classes that will eventually accommodate students/pupils who wish to enter the Public Schools including the creation of teaching positions to handle these classes as provided for in RA No. 5447 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01-A and 01-B.
Stop the practice of charging expenditures not within the purview of RA 5447 and the provisions of RA 7160.
Make proper representation for the payment of the services to the GF subject to the conditions provided under Sections 458(xi) of RA 7160.

We recommended that the MDRRM Officer adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the COA Audit Team of the Municipality the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and the Municipal Development Council.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant book up the transfer of the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF and other funds received from other sources intended to support disaster risk and management activities to its proper account code as required in the afore-cited Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer properly plan and program projects to carry out the purpose for which it was granted.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat / GSO Head submit to the Auditor a copy of all contracts, purchase orders and the like and each of the supporting documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from the execution of the contracts and issuance of purchase orders.
We also recommended that within 24 hours from acceptance of the goods or services being delivered, the official responsible for accepting the goods delivered should notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries.  In case of partial delivery of any item in the contract, the copy to be submitted shall indicate which items have been delivered and which were not.
We further recommended that a copy of the report of inspection or its equivalent be submitted to the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered and, in the case of unserviceable and disposable property/assets, immediately after the inspection thereof by management.

We recommended that the Local School Board stop the practice of hiring volunteer teachers and allowing them to teach in any grade levels, but to work instead for the organization and operation of extension classes that will eventually accommodate students/pupils who wish to enter the Public Schools including the creation of teaching positions to handle these classes as provided for in RA No. 5447 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01-A and 01-B.

They should see to it that activities to be funded by the SEF are included in the list of projects/activities that could be charged against the Special Education Fund pursuant to the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 7160 and Republic Act No. 5447.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board instruct /order the refund of Cash Gift paid to Local School Board Volunteer Teachers and Watchmen.
We likewise recommended that the Local School Board stop the practice of granting allowances and incentives to Job-hires and other individuals or group of people whose services are engaged on job orders, contracts of service, or of similar nature and where there is no employer-employee relationships and paid from non-personal services budgets, in the absence of statutory basis or specific legal authority.
